title: Can I Retire at 35 With $500k?
subtitle: Every strategy simulated — from the safest withdrawal plans to real estate, dividend income, and geo-arbitrage. No hand-waving. Just math.
description: Can you retire at 35 with $500k? See the math for U.S. costs, withdrawal rates, dividends, real estate, and geo-arbitrage.

faqs.question: Can I retire at 35 with $500k in the United States?
faqs.answer: For most people, retiring at 35 with $500k in the United States is not realistic because the portfolio must support 50 or more years of expenses, healthcare, taxes, and inflation.

faqs.question: How much income does $500k produce with the 4% rule?
faqs.answer: A $500k portfolio produces about $20,000 per year, or $1,667 per month, using a 4% withdrawal rate before taxes.

faqs.question: Can geo-arbitrage make retiring at 35 with $500k possible?
faqs.answer: Geo-arbitrage can make a $500k retirement more realistic if annual expenses fall low enough, especially in lower-cost countries or very low-cost domestic locations.
